data.summary: Unify existing Ledger and audit log into .fabric/events.jsonl as typed Event Ledger. MCP/server interfaces should automatically record typed events, including read-only query events where useful. correlation_id/session_id can be considered. Explicitly deprecate fab_append_intent and fab_update_registry as core interfaces. Rule text is source of truth; doctor/sync-meta handle baseline acceptance.

decisions: Read-only query events may be recorded
Do not prioritize human task summary in phase 1

data.summary: MCP tools are registered independently in packages/server/src/tools/* and call services directly; event writes are currently ad hoc in services: getRules->get_rules audit, getRuleSections->rule_selection audit, appendIntent->ledger plus edit_intent audit. planContext is readOnlyHint true but records no event; updateRegistry is destructive and records none. Event Ledger should introduce a shared recordEvent service and instrument service/tool lifecycle with event types for plan_context_requested, rule_sections_selected, intent_recorded/edit_intent_checked, registry_updated/deprecated, including correlation_id/session_id propagation.

sync-meta is the source-of-truth compiler for rule text: it reads .fabric/bootstrap/README.md plus .fabric/agents/**/*.md, computes node hash/stable_id/identity_source and revision, and --check-only fails on computed-vs-existing drift. doctor independently recomputes tracked file hashes/revision from agents.meta.json and reports stale meta/missing files/derived identity warnings, but does not sync baseline or write ledger. Existing doctor --fix only migrates legacy ledger. Event Ledger should add explicit rule_drift_detected on doctor audit/fix paths and rule_baseline_accepted when sync-meta updates agents.meta.json; normal doctor reads should stay non-mutating. Templates/docs still mention fab_update_registry as required, so deprecation must update bootstrap/pre-commit guidance and tests.

fab_append_intent/fab_update_registry are encoded as core user-facing protocol in server registration, protected-token lint, canonical bootstrap templates, installed lightweight bootstrap templates, docs, and hook warnings. Deprecation should keep/alias old MCP tools only for compatibility, remove them from bootstrap required-token policy, rewrite templates to prefer fab_plan_context+fab_get_rule_sections, sync-meta/doctor baseline acceptance, and typed events.jsonl recording, and update tests that inline old hard rules or assert current tool set.

Storage is split across .fabric/.intent-ledger.jsonl (+ legacy root fallback) and .fabric/audit.jsonl. read-ledger appends/reads LedgerEntry, skips kind=mcp-event lines, and doctor --fix only migrates legacy ledger; audit-log defines get_rules/edit_intent/rule_selection with a cursor-backed compliance window. HTTP MCP EventStore currently also writes kind=mcp-event into the ledger file. /api/ledger and history replay consume readLedger; /events watches ledger/meta/lock/forensic files and emits projected SSE events with ring-buffer replay, but does not watch audit.jsonl. Migration should introduce .fabric/events.jsonl plus projections/compat readers for ledger views, audit compliance windows, MCP replay, and SSE ledger:appended semantics.

Created Event Ledger foundation: exported shared typed event schema with fabric-event envelope, schema_version=1, id/ts, optional correlation_id/session_id, and event_type discriminators for rule_context_planned, rule_selection, rule_sections_fetched, edit_intent_checked, rule_drift_detected, rule_baseline_accepted, and mcp_event. Added server .fabric/events.jsonl constants plus append/read helpers with malformed-line tolerance and filters. Legacy .intent-ledger.jsonl and audit.jsonl callers/files remain untouched.

Projected legacy LedgerEntry and AuditLogEntry views from .fabric/events.jsonl while preserving legacy fallback reads. readLedger merges legacy .intent-ledger rows with edit_intent_checked projections, keeping richer legacy duplicates. readAuditLog merges audit.jsonl with Event Ledger rule_context_planned/rule_selection/edit_intent_checked projections and window filtering. Audit appenders now dual-write typed Event Ledger records. SSE watches .fabric/events.jsonl and emits compatible ledger:appended frames; HTTP MCP EventStore stores mcp_event records in Event Ledger and falls back to legacy mcp-event rows for replay.

Instrumented MCP/service boundaries with typed Event Ledger records: planContext emits rule_context_planned with target paths/stable ids/diagnostics and optional correlation/session ids; getRuleSections emits rule_selection plus rule_sections_fetched with requested sections and diagnostics; legacy getRules and appendIntent/audit compatibility paths propagate correlation/session metadata into typed audit-compatible events. Deprecated tool registrations remain in place and no fab_append_intent dependency was added.

Doctor report now exposes metaDriftDetails with file/stable_id/expected_hash/actual_hash and remains non-mutating. doctor --fix accepts existing tracked rule hash drift, updates agents.meta.json revision, invalidates meta cache, and records rule_drift_detected, rule_baseline_accepted, and baseline_synced events. sync-meta records baseline acceptance/sync events when it rewrites compiled meta, including drift detail for changed tracked files. Vitest rejects --runInBand in this repo, so targeted suites were verified with compatible commands.

Deprecated fab_append_intent and fab_update_registry as user-facing core surfaces while retaining compatibility registration. Bootstrap/protected-token policy now requires fab_plan_context, fab_get_rule_sections, and .fabric/events.jsonl; generated CLI/bootstrap/templates point to automatic typed Event Ledger records plus fabric sync-meta/fabric doctor --fix baseline acceptance. Server registration tests assert deprecated descriptions for legacy mutation tools.

Final compatibility sweep redirected remaining compatibility write paths to .fabric/events.jsonl: appendLedgerEntry now writes edit_intent_checked events and appendIntent relies on compliance telemetry instead of legacy ledger append; audit appenders no longer write .fabric/audit.jsonl. Legacy ledger/audit readers and migration tests remain intentional fallback coverage. Docs/ADR/spec now describe Event Ledger as primary. Grep remaining old-file/deprecated-tool references are compatibility code/tests or guidance saying not required. pnpm test -- --runInBand is unsupported by this monorepo; package suites were verified with direct Vitest commands.
tests: cd packages/server && pnpm exec vitest run
cd packages/shared && pnpm exec vitest run
